reply_text: In Korean society, showing respect for elders is paramount. This tradition reflects the Confucian emphasis on a hierarchical social structure and the honor due to those who are older and wiser. When a younger person backs away from an elder, they are deliberately maintaining eye contact and demonstrating that their full attention and deference are on the elder until the very end of the interaction. Turning your back would be considered highly disrespectful, a non-verbal sign of dismissal. This gesture is a powerful, non-verbal way of saying, "You are so important that I will not take my eyes off you."
